questions.text: The fundamental theorem of arithmetic states that every composite number can be expressed as a product of:
questions.explanation: Every composite number can be uniquely factorized into primes, apart from the order of factors.

questions.text: If two positive integers a and b are written as a = x³y² and b = xy³, where x, y are prime numbers, then HCF (a, b) is:
questions.explanation: HCF is the product of the smallest power of each common prime factor: x¹ and y².

questions.text: The LCM of the smallest prime number and the smallest composite number is:
questions.explanation: Smallest prime is 2, smallest composite is 4. LCM(2, 4) = 4.

questions.text: If HCF(306, 657) = 9, what is the LCM(306, 657)?
The number √3 is a/an:
questions.explanation: √3 cannot be expressed in p/q form where p, q are integers and q≠0.

questions.text: For any two positive integers a and b, HCF(a, b) × LCM(a, b) is equal to:
questions.explanation: The product of HCF and LCM of two numbers is always equal to the product of the numbers themselves.

questions.text: If n is any natural number, then 6ⁿ can never end with the digit:
questions.explanation: For a number to end with 0, its prime factorization must contain both 2 and 5. 6ⁿ = (2 × 3)ⁿ, which lacks 5.

questions.text: The decimal expansion of 17/8 is:
A rational number in its lowest terms p/q has a terminating decimal expansion if the prime factorization of q is of the form (where n, m are non-negative integers):
questions.explanation: If the denominator has factors other than 2 and 5, the expansion is non-terminating repeating.

questions.text: Is (3 + √23) - √23 a rational or irrational number?
questions.explanation: The expression simplifies to 3, which is a rational number.

questions.text: The product of a non-zero rational and an irrational number is:
questions.explanation: A non-zero rational multiplied by an irrational number is always irrational.

questions.text: HCF of 92 and 510 is:
questions.explanation: 92 = 2² × 23; 510 = 2 × 3 × 5 × 17. The only common factor is 2.

questions.text: Which of the following is not irrational?
The sum of exponents of prime factors in the prime factorization of 196 is:
questions.explanation: 196 = 2² × 7². Sum of exponents = 2 + 2 = 4.

questions.text: If p is a prime number and p divides a² (where a is a positive integer), then p divides:
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental theorem used to prove the irrationality of numbers like √2.

questions.text: What is the HCF of 135 and 225?
LCM of 12, 15 and 21 is:
Express 156 as a product of its primes.
Total number of factors of a prime number is:
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself.

questions.text: Decimal expansion of 23 / (2³ 5²) will terminate after how many places?
questions.explanation: It terminates after the number of places equal to the highest exponent of 2 or 5 in the denominator.

questions.text: The HCF of two co-prime numbers is always:
questions.explanation: Co-prime numbers are numbers that have only 1 as a common factor.

questions.text: If HCF(a, b) = 12 and a × b = 1800, then LCM(a, b) is:
questions.explanation: LCM = Product / HCF = 1800 / 12 = 150.

questions.text: The number 3 × 5 × 7 + 7 is a:
questions.explanation: 7(3×5 + 1) = 7 × 16 = 112. It is composite and even, making 2 and 3 false, wait. 112 is composite and even.

questions.text: Which of the following is a rational number?
questions.explanation: √9 = 3, which can be written as 3/1, a rational number.

questions.text: If the HCF of 65 and 117 is expressible in the form 65m - 117, then the value of m is:
The exponent of 2 in the prime factorization of 144 is:
If p₁ and p₂ are two odd prime numbers such that p₁ > p₂, then p₁² - p₂² is:
questions.explanation: Square of an odd number is odd. Odd - Odd = Even.

questions.text: The largest number which divides 70 and 125, leaving remainders 5 and 8 respectively, is:
questions.explanation: Calculate HCF of (70-5) and (125-8), i.e., HCF(65, 117) = 13.

questions.text: The decimal expansion of the rational number 14587 / 1250 will terminate after:
questions.explanation: 1250 = 2 × 5⁴. Since the highest power is 4, it terminates after 4 places.

questions.text: If HCF(a, b) = 1, then a and b are called:
questions.explanation: Co-prime numbers have no common factor other than 1.

questions.text: Is π (pi) a rational or irrational number?
questions.explanation: While 22/7 is a rational approximation, the actual value of π is non-terminating and non-repeating.

questions.text: The HCF of the smallest composite number and the smallest two-digit composite number is:
questions.explanation: Smallest composite is 4. Smallest 2-digit composite is 10. HCF(4, 10) = 2. Wait, 4 and 10... HCF is 2.

questions.text: If two positive integers p and q can be expressed as p = ab² and q = a³b; a, b being prime numbers, then LCM (p, q) is:
questions.explanation: LCM is the product of the highest power of each prime factor involved in the numbers.

questions.text: The values of x and y in the given factor tree are: [x] -> 3, [y] -> 3, 7
What is the HCF of two consecutive even numbers?
questions.explanation: Consecutive even numbers like 4,6 or 10,12 always have a common factor of 2.

questions.text: What is the HCF of two consecutive odd numbers?
questions.explanation: Consecutive odd numbers like 7,9 or 15,17 are always co-prime.

questions.text: If HCF(26, 169) = 13, then LCM(26, 169) is:
Which of the following is a terminating decimal?
questions.explanation: 8 = 2³, which is in the form 2ⁿ 5ᵐ. Others have prime factors other than 2 or 5 in the denominator.
The sum of a rational and irrational numbers is irrational.

questions.text: The LCM of two numbers is 1200. Which of the following cannot be their HCF?
questions.explanation: The HCF of two numbers must always be a factor of their LCM. 500 is not a factor of 1200.
